programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Otto surveys the condition of his rusting barge after a winter in drydock. Elsewhere, Eivin and Eve use explosives to clear land for a new root cellar

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: A broken-down bulldozer sends Jeremy on an airborne cross-country mission, and Jenny finds herself facing off with a fierce predator as she is stalked in the wild by a wolf

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Jeremy must enter an uneasy alliance with an old enemy as he makes plans to expand his farmstead, and Neil tackles some major upgrades as he prepares to sell the town

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: Specialists armed with the latest technology set out to locate the fabled bigfoot. The start of their search takes them to the forests of Oregon

programme.title: Swimming: Phelps v Shark
programme.desc: Documentary in which former Olympic swimmer Michael Phelps tests his skills in the water by competing in a race against a shark - one of the worlds fastest marine predators

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: A game of underwater copycat shows how smart dolphins really are. Plus, how hyenas can eat pretty much anything and geckos ability to apparently defy gravity

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How humpback whales are able to leap so far out of the water, why dogs tilt their heads and how do spiders shed their own skin. Plus, why do animals gather in swarms?

programme.desc: The Irwins caravan into the Outback to release baby kangaroos and an emu onto their wildlife conservation area - but ffind more animals in need of rescue

programme.desc: Game Warden Mike Boone teams up with local police to search for armed suspects after a robbery. Plus, a family are investigated for illegally keeping a pet deer

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff fights to save a bearded dragon that is having a seizure, and Dr Amy is put to the test when a routine cat neuter turns complicated

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff must act fast to save a kitten with head trauma, while Dr Amy treats a dog with a weak heart. A passer-by helps a family when their dog is hit by a car

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: A stray dog found under a home turns out to be terrified of humans. A couple who have visited Villalobos before want to adopt a dog that is comfortable on a boat

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Lizzy and Elise struggle to rescue a dangerously dehydrated dog. An abandoned and anxious pooch is moved to a new warehouse for canines with special needs

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps a man with Crohns disease who is worried about his cats constant moaning, and also diagnoses the problems of an old-age feline

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy looks at a cat who is coming between two lovers. Meanwhile, Jon and Michelles marriage is riddled with stress over their angry feline Zooey

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson and his gang ride out to build a double-decker hideout in Texas Hill Country, featuring a saloon-style first floor

programme.title: Wild Costa Rica
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the huge numbers of olive ridley sea turtles that migrate annually to Costa Rica: a country also home to capuchin, howler monkeys and the Hercules beetle

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: The birds that can fly as high as a passenger jet, and why tiny jumping spiders are dancing for their lives. Plus, how West Coast squirrels have outsmarted rattlesnakes

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.sub-title: The Secret to a Long Life
programme.desc: How homing pigeons are able to find their way home, how spiders lure bugs into their trap, and how some fish can survive for years above ground

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: Bradley and Billy are in the Mojave Desert, California, to find the bombardier beetle. When attacked or agitated, it releases a hot, noxious chemical spray

programme.title: Little Giants
programme.desc: In the Madagascan rainforest, the team searches for a nearly invisible creature, the panther chameleon. When testing, the reptile seems to defy the laws of physics

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: The Browns face impossible odds as they seek the final pieces for their off the grid dream. Noah misses his girl, while Bam has a surprise announcement

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: A chocolate factory gears up for its annual Holiday Cactus Lighting event and ask Wayde and Brett to build the ultimate festive aquarium

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: In preparation for the spring cattle drive, Atz Lee, Otto and August survey the homestead for signs of bears, and use modern surveillance to track their movement

programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: The intrepid anglers are in Borneo searching for the mahseer - a fish close to extinction. To find it, they risk entering the territory of the headhunting Dayak people

programme.title: Jaws of the Deep
programme.desc: Marine biologist Greg Skomal and the Remus SharkCam team return to Guadalupe to find the worlds largest great white shark, Deep Blue

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: With a team member down, Russell and Mireya must forge ahead to hunt down leads, and their investigations uncover their first pieces of potential evidence

programme.title: Monster Tag
programme.desc: Top athletes including Olympic champion, Lindsey Vonn, team up with scientists to learn about the oceans top predators, using the latest satellite tagging technology

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How is one fish blowing scientists away with its brainpower? How have dogs learnt to melt our hearts? Do chimps laugh at the same jokes we do?

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Why domestic cats have trouble getting down from trees and why kiwi birds lay monster-sized eggs compared to their size

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: An Italian familys bond is affected by cat Maxs behaviour. Plus, a woman moves in with a friend not knowing that the resident feline has psychotic tendencies

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Two best friends and room-mates clash when one is terrorised by the others cat, Buddha. Plus, a couple take to sleeping in separate beds because of troublesome feline Dylan

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: The officers respond to a call at a livery stable and find themselves battling to save the life of a badly neglected horse

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: The latest woman in Timothys life has a jealous ex-boyfriend who does not take kindly to him trying to steal her away. Ronnie Corbett stars

programme.title: Nature and Us: A History Through Art
programme.desc: James Fox tells the story of the human races ever-changing relationship with nature through the lens of some of the worlds most extraordinary artworks, beginning with a look back at the art of the ancient world, looking at the art of prehistoric hunters, the advent of agriculture, the first cities, and the beginnings of Hinduism and Christianity

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.sub-title: A Rose by Any Other Name
programme.desc: A middle-aged widows family become convinced a photographer is only marrying her for money and ask Hetty to investigate. Given a week to probe his past and find out whether hes a gold-digging cad or a 24-carat gentleman, the unlikely sleuth unearths some extremely interesting facts. Drama based on the novel by David Cook, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents the silver jubilee edition of the old-time music hall programme from January 1978, with Ken Dodd, Jan Hunt and members of the Players Theatre, London, taking to the stage of the Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Wildlife on One
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the story of an abandoned baby grey squirrel found by chance in the Oxfordshire countryside. First broadcast in 1981

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Author Patrick Wright explores the architecture of Dorset, including the Church of St Peter and St Paul in Blandford, Milton Abbey, and the Old Higher Lighthouse at Portland

programme.title: Wild
programme.desc: Bill Oddie narrates this look at the behaviour of stoats, as cameras capture the exploits of a female raising her young in the grounds of the 18th-century mansion in Derbyshire

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: Water has always been the driving force in East Anglia - its ebb and flow has shaped the fenland. The ancient wetlands once covered hundreds of square miles, but today, well over 90 per cent has been drained and converted into farmland. Every winter, thousands of whooper swans return to the flooded pastures of the Ouse washes from their Arctic breeding grounds. On the Welney Wetlands, spring sees mad march hares boxing over mates. It was once thought these bouts were male hares boxing for dominance, but it is often the females throwing the punches to fend off the attentions of over-eager suitors. Narrated by Toby Jones

programme.title: The Bridges That Built London with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The historian explores the citys Thames crossings, discovering there is much more to them than simply a means of getting from one place to another. Beginning in the marshes of the estuary, he moves upstream to Vauxhall, where archaeologists have recorded Londons oldest bridge, and also learns about the legend of London Bridge, before reaching journeys end at the Millennium Bridge near St Pauls Cathedral

programme.title: Janet Suzman Remembers - Hedda Gabler
programme.desc: The actress looks back on her acclaimed portrayal of Henrik Ibsens formidable heroine in a 1972 BBC adaptation, directed by Waris Hussein and also starring Ian McKellen as Heddas insufferable husband Tesman, with Jane Asher as childhood friend Thea. Reflecting on Heddas fascinating complexity, emotional depth and human flaws, Suzman describes the task of stepping fully into a characters worldview

programme.title: Ibsen: Hedda Gabler
programme.desc: Michael Meyers 1972 adaptation of Henrik Ibsens play about a newlywed woman who is feeling trapped and dangerous. Janet Suzman and Ian McKellen star

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe reveals why breathing is so important to becoming still and being in the moment, illustrated with natural history footage

programme.title: The Bridges That Built London with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The historian explores the citys Thames crossings, discovering there is much more to them than simply a means of getting from one place to another. Beginning in the marshes of the estuary, he moves upstream to Vauxhall, where archaeologists have recorded Londons oldest bridge, and also learns about the legend of London Bridge, before reaching journeys end at the Millennium Bridge near St Pauls Cathedral

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Author Patrick Wright explores the architecture of Dorset, including the Church of St Peter and St Paul in Blandford, Milton Abbey, and the Old Higher Lighthouse at Portland

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: Water has always been the driving force in East Anglia - its ebb and flow has shaped the fenland. The ancient wetlands once covered hundreds of square miles, but today, well over 90 per cent has been drained and converted into farmland. Every winter, thousands of whooper swans return to the flooded pastures of the Ouse washes from their Arctic breeding grounds. On the Welney Wetlands, spring sees mad march hares boxing over mates. It was once thought these bouts were male hares boxing for dominance, but it is often the females throwing the punches to fend off the attentions of over-eager suitors. Narrated by Toby Jones

programme.title: Golf: The Players Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the prestigious tournament at TPC Sawgrass in Ponte Vedra Beach, Florida, which saw Rory McIlroy triumph in 2025 to claim this title for the second time in his career. The Northern Irishman defeated JJ Spaun in a three-hole aggregate play-off after both players finished on an overall score of 12-under-par after four rounds of play

programme.title: Irelands Treasures Uncovered
programme.desc: Documentary about the artefacts that have helped to shape and create modern Ireland, both north and south. Alice Roberts and Gavin Hughes uncover the tales behind treasures such as the Tara Brooch, the Broighter Hoard, the Waterford Charter Roll and others

programme.title: Call My Bluff
programme.desc: Robert Robinson presides over another duel of words and wits as team captains Frank Muir and Patrick Campbell are joined by guests Donald Churchill, Pauline Yates, Joanna Lumley and Frank Kearton. First broadcast in 1976

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Bernard Levin and Robin Ray. With guest musician Henryk Szeryng. First broadcast in 1979

programme.title: Imagine
programme.sub-title: Tracey Emin: Where Do You Draw the Line?
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the artist over the course of 12 months to chart her creative process, talking about her life to date and how she sees the rest of her career. Tracey discusses her troubled early years in Margate and the series of breakthroughs in the 1990s that saw her become a leading light of the Young British Artists, featuring career-defining work like My Bed and her embroidered tent Everyone I Have Ever Slept With 1963-1995. Contributors include Nicholas Serota, Jay Jopling, Maria Balshaw and David Dawson

programme.title: Emin/Munch: Between the Clock and the Bed
programme.desc: Edvard Munchs work has long inspired Tracey Emin, from her early video piece Homage to Edvard Munch and All My Dead Children to her monumental nine-metre high bronze sculpture The Mother. This documentary charts the affinities between Munchs art and Emins, exploring the ways that this godfather of expressionism resonates with one of Britains pre-eminent artists

programme.title: Nature and Us: A History Through Art
programme.desc: James Fox uses art to explore how humans began to try to understand nature for the very first time. From the Song dynasty in China and the Islamic world, through to the Scientific Revolution and the advent of the industrial era, he shows the very different ways in which humans came to both appreciate and understand nature

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe reveals why breathing is so important to becoming still and being in the moment, illustrated with natural history footage

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Bernard Levin and Robin Ray. With guest musician Henryk Szeryng. First broadcast in 1979

programme.title: Irelands Treasures Uncovered
programme.desc: Documentary about the artefacts that have helped to shape and create modern Ireland, both north and south. Alice Roberts and Gavin Hughes uncover the tales behind treasures such as the Tara Brooch, the Broighter Hoard, the Waterford Charter Roll and others

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Manchester City at London Stadium and Chelsea v Newcastle United at Stamford Bridge. City claimed a comfortable 3-0 win over the Hammers in Decembers reverse fixture, and Chelsea came back from 2-0 down at halt time to earn a 2-2 draw against the Magpies. Plus, Burnley v Bournemouth, Sunderland v Brighton and Hove Albion, and Arsenal v Everton

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: Interviews with key politicians and public figures

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: Seven well-known personalities with differing faiths and beliefs put on their backpacks and waterproofs and set out travel more than 1,600km on foot and by road and sea to learn about St Columba, a key figure in early British Christianity. Following ancient paths and heritage walking trails are interior designer Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, former England cricketer Monty Panesar, actress Louisa Clein, TV presenters Nick Hewer and Scarlett Moffatt, comedian Shazia Mirza and Paralympian Will Bayley

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt celebrates the Muslim festival Eid al-Fitr, joined by chefs Dina Macki, Ilhan Mohamed Abdi and Naeem Mohamed, and guests Faraaz Noor, Sabrina Elba and Ola Labib

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Builth Wells in mid Wales. Two teams battle it out at the Royal Welsh Showground in Powys, with experts Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks on hand to help

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: To mark Mothering Sunday, Claire McCollum is in Liverpool celebrating 150 years of Mothers Union and meeting mums inspired by faith to show Gods love and care to those in need

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester United (Kick-off 2.15pm). All the action from the showpiece match, held at Ashton Gate in Bristol. This is the seventh successive season in which Chelsea have reached the final of this competition, and they lifted the trophy last term with a 2-1 win over Manchester City. In contrast, this is the first time United have reached the final of this competition, and should they prevail itll be only their second major honour, with their first coming two seasons ago when they won the FA Cup

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh host the first of this years semi-finals. Over five gruelling events and the energy-draining Eliminator, only the fastest and strongest will make it to the grand final. The referees are on point in Collision, unbeaten Comet returns to Hang Tough, and Cyclone gets bored on top of Everest

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Anita Rani head to Devon for a special lambing season episode. From scanning ewes for pregnancy, to the rush of new births, and bottle-feeding vulnerable lambs through their early days, Adam and Anita look at all stages of lambing, following the teamwork, care and expertise that bring new life safely into the world in a county shaped by generations of sheep farming

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Thirlestane Castle in the Scottish Borders, where treasures include a Rod Stewart Gold Disc a silver chalice thought to have been found in a potato field, and a gold, enamelled snuffbox gifted by a Russian Tsar. Fiona also looks into the history of the famous Paisley shawl, and reveals the personal stories behind three antiques belonging to Silverware Expert Gordon Foster

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Period drama based on Janice Hadlows novel, telling the story of the overlooked middle sister in Pride and Prejudice, both during the events of Jane Austens classic novel and beyond. The Meryton assembly brings Marys first taste of romance, but also leaves her forced to decide between trying to please her family and forging a path of her own. Starring Ella Bruccoleri and Richard E Grant

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: After disappointment at the ball, Mary sets aside hopes of finding a match and devotes herself to more serious pursuits. But Marys resolve is tested when Mr Bennets distant cousin, Mr Collins, visits Longbourn with a surprising proposal. Mary contemplates a decision which could save her family from ruin, but is she really willing to suppress her true feelings to secure her familys future?

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: More video evidence of the attack emerges. Careys assertion that an insidious conspiracy is unfolding at the heart of the establishment gains little traction, so she takes matters into her own hands and seeks an unlikely ally in Gemma Garland. Meanwhile, Noah Pierson finds his first day at work more surprising than he ever expected

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Tottenham Hotspur at Anfield, and Manchester United v Aston Villa at Old Trafford. Liverpool won 2-1 against a Spurs side that ended the game with nine men in the reverse fixture, but the Londoners will have been determined to avoid another defeat to ease their relegation concerns. Villa beat United by the same score when they last met, with Morgan Rogers scoring a goal in either half to inspire them to victory. Plus, Crystal Palace v Leeds United, and Nottingham Forest v Fulham

programme.title: The Womens Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekends matches, including Chelsea v Manchester United in the League Cup final, and Aston Villa v Manchester City in the Super League

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: From Strictly to the sofa, Claudia kicks off her chat show which promises Hollywood actors, the best of British and comedy stars - all put at their ease by the hosts trademark warmth, charm and humour. Her first guests are Hollywood star and musician Jeff Goldblum, actress Vanessa Williams, comedy icon Jennifer Saunders and funnyman Tom Allen

programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Crimewatch Live
programme.desc: In 2005, two best friends died in an arson attack while sleeping, but it remains to be seen who lit the fire. Plus, a manhunt across continents to catch a dangerous criminal

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A first-time developer transforms a terrace property in Stoke-on-Trent, while more seasoned developers tackle projects in Leeds and Rugby in Warwickshire. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Nick Hall help the teams in Staffordshire, and Christina Trevanion discovers the story of a First World War stretcher bearer during the Battle of the Somme

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: Ben wants to drop out of university to work full time on his online gaming channel, but his parents worry hes rushing into a decision. Bear and his team set the family a blindfolded navigation task to see how they communicate under pressure and a map-reading challenge. In a campfire chat, mediator Mo tries to help the family understand each others perspectives

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden revamps the second guest bedroom, while Alan Carr gets handed the short straw - the windowless bathroom. An old dividing wall that cuts a window in half is demolished and their efforts are rewarded when loads of natural light floods in. Away from the day job, Alan ropes in Amanda for a trip to one of Granadas famous hammam spas for some much-needed inspiration and RandR. The duo also head to Seville for a lesson in silk painting to create some statement bedside lampshades before arriving in one of Malagas top karaoke joints

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps globetrotter Sabiha find an Argyll and Bute retreat, with a line-up that includes a magical home on an island, just a short ferry ride from the mainland. He also meets a couple who made their own Argyll and Bute dream come true when they moved to a wonderful lochside croft

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea steps inside Glasgows Britannia Panopticon, the worlds oldest-surviving music hall, to help piano technician Jamie revive a 1904 Nilson pianola with roots in a New York speakeasy. Will Kirk is in Inverness to collect Debbies handcrafted chess-set, a family heirloom thats requires Kirsten Ramsays expert touch if its to be played with again

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong and Ed Gamble

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Its Mothers Day in Albert Square, and Lauren and Peter prepare to celebrate with the Beales at No 45. An upset Linda prepares to celebrate the day in the Vic with Elaine as Johnny is still distant with her, but things soon go from bad to worse when shes forced to invite Bea. Jean arrives back in Walford, but its clear there is still friction between her and Zoe over Jasmine

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: The singer and his nephew take a trip along the Atlantic coast, starting in Cork. They go foraging for edible plants and face off in the ancient sport of road bowling along the back roads of the port town of Castletown Berehaven. They also visit an enormous rewilding project area and on the Beara Peninsula and the countrys first Tibetan Buddhist Temple and Retreat Centre

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: BBC Medical Editor Fergus Walsh reports on the future of assisted dying in the UK, travelling to Western Australia, where voluntary assisted dying has been available since 2021 to meet one of the doctors who pioneered it, and to a patient preparing for an assisted death. He also talks to leading supporters and opponents in the UK, and visits a hospice to talk to patients at the end of their lives

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The semi-finals begin, with just eight contenders in the running. The first task requires them to deliver a no-waste dish, that makes use of every part of their chosen ingredients. With the four best dishes winning a place in the next round, the remaining chefs are challenged to produce a vegetarian meal

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Aontas (2025)
programme.desc: Drama about three unlikely thieves who make a disastrous attempt to hold up a rural Irish Credit Union. The story unfolds in reverse, going backwards through their recent lives revealing the circumstances that led them down this path and that caused their heist to go so disastrously wrong. Starring Carrie Crowley, Brid Brennan and Eva-Jane Gaffney

programme.title: Trying
programme.desc: During an activity break with the children, Nikki worries that she hasnt been able to bond with them as well as Jason has. Karen realises that she hates her job

programme.title: Trying
programme.desc: Nikki and Jason prepare for Tylers seventh birthday party, but he is overwhelmed by all the attention. Karen finds a novel way to tell Scott he is going to be a father

programme.title: The Weakest Link

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet tries to dump Jonny, but suffers the indignity of being upstaged by some pasties. Louise gets revenge on David, and Donna plucks up the courage to pursue a new dream. Sitcom, starring Ralf Little, Will Mellor, Natalie Casey, Kathryn Drysdale and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny finds a new girlfriend, Gaz challenges David to a drinking contest, and a book has a profound effect on Louise. Sitcom, starring Ralf Little, Natalie Casey, Kathryn Drysdale and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny ruins his first date with Kate by trying to be too macho. A large spider spoils Louises plans to live with Janet, and Gaz tries to become a pop idol. Sitcom, starring Ralf Little, Natalie Casey, Will Mellor and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Lonely Janet and frisky Gaz grow closer than intended and Louise applies for a new job. Jonny and Kate learn how biscuits can spice things up in the bedroom. Comedy, starring Ralf Little, Kathryn Drysdale and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommys recovery is back on track after he and John put his hand to the test. The pair are training again, and the search for Tommys next opponent begins. Eager to make the most of family time, Tommy splashes out on a £90,000 motor home for family trips with Molly and Bambi and takes his mother out for lunch and a long-overdue catch-up. Tommy reveals that John has had a recent health scare, so to cheer him up, father and son take their very different caravans to a local steam fair

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Brothers, flatmates and polar opposites Samir and Nikhil find themselves on an adventure when they accidentally steal a package from their maniac neighbour. Short, one-off comedy, starring Karan Gill, Ikky Kabir, Eshaan Akbar, Elina Saleh, Ed Kear and Andrea Valls

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: Zara McDermott discovers the dark side of Thailands backpacker culture. In the first episode, she lands in trouble with the Thai police when she starts to uncover the scale of Thailands illegal sex industry. A visit to the Bangkok Immigration Detention Centre exposes how easy it is to get caught on the wrong side of the law

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Under pressure from the gang to get rid of the informant, Mark searches for Ravi before finding him at the Boxing Den. Lauren worries about her future, as news reaches her that the car lot is up for sale, and Phil avoids a situation

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Priya panics about Ravis safety, and heads to find Vicki to reveal all. Following their respective conversations with Peter and Jack, its clear that Max and Lauren both have very different ideas on how their business partnerships is going to run

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Priya is concerned for Ravis mental wellbeing, but Ravi heads to the gym to avoid her questioning. In the cafe, Ross tries to reassure Vicki of her safety, but their mood is soured when Mark arrives and chastises them both for getting involved. Peter supports Lauren as she prepares for her first day as manager of the car lot, but she is upset to learn that Max intends to run the business

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lauren desperately tries to prove herself by selling a classic car, and when a thug attacks her, Mark rushes to Laurens aid. Vicki asks Phil for help, and Oscar leads a smear campaign on Ian at the request of Elaine. However, Beale soon rumbles their plan

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and her team of experts are back to help more families declutter their homes. They begin with the Kings in Fife - retired architectural technician Gerry, care worker Trish and two of their three grown-up children. Their house contains 30 years of memories, knick-knacks and general clutter - not to mention countless unfinished DIY projects. During the process, the team learn Gerry has recently been diagnosed with early onset dementia, so it is important they hold on to items that will continue to spark memories for him while losing any clutter so it is easy for him to move around

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Comedy about five talented black teenagers at a prestigious boarding school. As the boarders gear up for their final term, a shocking act of vandalism throws everything into chaos. With the school on lockdown, Toby has to find a way to break out and see Abby. Leah is under suspicion for the school destruction, and Femi is distracted by a mystery girl. Jaheim wrestles with a dilemma that will change everything

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: The students are teamed up with career mentors, but things quickly go wrong. Leah is fuming with Felix about the sabotage, and Femis dreams are crushed. Omar is in denial, and Toby is distracted by Abbys dangerous games. Violence erupts on the hockey pitch, and the students bright futures feel further away than ever

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: This week, the makeover challenge goes Olympic as the queens drag up a host of Team GB Olympians, including Greg Rutherford, Shanaze Reade, Molly Thompson-Smith, Celia Quansah and Daniel Jervis. Pop superstar Anastacia joins RuPaul on the judging panel alongside Michelle Visage and Graham Norton

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise

In the first episode, she lands in trouble with the Thai police when she starts to uncover the scale of Thailands illegal sex industry. A visit to the Bangkok Immigration Detention Centre exposes how easy it is to get caught on the wrong side of the law

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: Zara McDermott meets men chasing cheap thrills in Asias sex capital, Pattaya, and also talks to a local sex worker, who reveals the harsh reality of working in the illegal sex industry. She then heads to Ko Phangan, an exotic island famed for its spirituality and beauty. For Zara, this is paradise - but Lucio, a Brit who built a burgeoning cannabis business here while the drug was decriminalised, reveals that navigating the local law isnt always easy

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Comedy about five talented black teenagers at a prestigious boarding school. As the boarders gear up for their final term, a shocking act of vandalism throws everything into chaos. With the school on lockdown, Toby has to find a way to break out and see Abby. Leah is under suspicion for the school destruction, and Femi is distracted by a mystery girl. Jaheim wrestles with a dilemma that will change everything

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Waterloo Road
programme.desc: Pregnant student Zoe causes problems for Jack, while Andrews new scheme to bring a little competition into the school backfires. Lorna considers having an abortion because of Toms apparent disinterest in being a father, and Chlo visits Donte in prison, where they discuss what will happen if Holly wakes from her coma and reveals the truth about the crash. Drama, starring Jason Merrells

programme.title: Waterloo Road
programme.desc: Izzies ex-husband Jimmy causes chaos at the school when he demands access to their children, and Tom confronts Lorna about her miscarriage. Meanwhile, the pupils set up a homework scam to gain more points in Andrews new house system, but it isnt long before their devious plot is uncovered. Jack wonders about the sudden improvement in the grades gained by Stephs French class

programme.title: The Jetty
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring Jenna Coleman as a detective who uncovers a dark web of secrets in the sleepy town shes always called home. Recently widowed DC Ember Manning suspects there is a predator targeting underage girls. She finds herself reflecting on the age difference between herself and her late husband

programme.title: The Jetty
programme.desc: Still reeling from her shocking discovery, Ember is desperate to find out what Riz knows about the Amy Knightly case. They begin to share information and Ember tells Riz her suspicions about the identity of Mirandas abuser. Ember is horrified when shes told that the lake is to be searched for Amys remains. When Riz stops returning Embers calls, Ember suspects that its because she has uncovered the terrible truth about what happened to Amy. Crime drama, starring Jenna Coleman and Weruche Opia

programme.title: Caitlin Hanna: Trafficked in Belfast
programme.desc: Documentary about the life and sudden death of a young Belfast woman and how it fuelled a three-year police investigation that exposed a hidden criminal underworld operating in Northern Ireland. Twenty-one-year-old Caitins life was tragically cut short, leaving her mother Deborah and her friends searching for answers. What they didnt know was that Caitlin has been exploited by a criminal enterprise, where she and many other young women were groomed, controlled and fed drugs by ruthless men

programme.title: Men of the Manosphere
programme.desc: James Blake enters the virtual world of manhood, often referred to as the manosphere, in search of status, wealth and relationships. He meets three young men who have turned to male social media influencers for guidance and instruction as they seek to better themselves and achieve success in different areas of their lives, both on and offline

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy realises just how far he and Molly have come since the separation, but Johns worries grow as Tommy takes on a 100km triathlon, pushing his body to new physical limits. Before the challenge, Tommy takes some downtime at the spa with his mates, preparing for a huge press push ahead of his series premiere. After completing a series of intense physical challenges, he has to deal with a social media backlash

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives news of the untimely death of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, which reminds him to live life to its fullest. He then sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible - climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed

programme.title: The Jetty
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring Jenna Coleman as a detective who uncovers a dark web of secrets in the sleepy town shes always called home. Recently widowed DC Ember Manning suspects there is a predator targeting underage girls. She finds herself reflecting on the age difference between herself and her late husband

programme.title: The Jetty

When Riz stops returning Embers calls, Ember suspects that its because she has uncovered the terrible truth about what happened to Amy. Crime drama, starring Jenna Coleman and Weruche Opia

programme.title: New York, New York (1977)
programme.desc: An egotistical saxophonist starts a turbulent love affair with an up-and-coming vocalist at the end of World War Two as they both struggle to make it in the Big Band era. Martin Scorseses musical drama, starring Robert De Niro, Liza Minnelli, Lionel Stander, Barry Primus and Georgie Auld

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The reetective drama, starring Holliday Grainger. Whistleblower Rachel Carey is ready to launch a revolutionary new camera network capable of catching live deep-fakes in action. With the home secretary Isaac Turners support, the changes signal a new dawn in UK counter-terrorism policing, at least until a terror attack in central London sends shockwaves through the nation, leaving Rachel the one key eyewitness

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Take a Hike
programme.desc: Life coach Teresa hosts a wild walk in Kielder Forest. It remains to be seen whether her traditional Northumbrian picnic will find her in pole position by the end

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Vick Hope explore sugar beet harvesting in the east of England, 100 years after sugar was first grown in this country. In Norfolk, Vick meets farmers racing to lift the last of this years crop, while Charlotte visits one of the countrys biggest processing plants. Plus, Tom Heap investigates whats really in the sewage sludge spread as fertiliser

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: My Kind of Town
programme.desc: Ian Hamilton and his guide dog Major head to the West Lothian town of Bathgate, which has an incredible industrial past and now a popular commuter area for people working in Edinburgh. Ian helps out in an award-winning butchers and offers to do some taste testing, then works it off by climbing a hill to find some neolithic remains. He investigates the towns historic theatre and meets a local brass band before getting the chance to relax in Scotlands biggest floatation centre

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from central England must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang are joined by acclaimed film producer, Bafta winner and Oscar nominee Alison Owen. Only one of the chefs will triumph and go through to represent central England at the national finals. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Lover Come Back (1961)
programme.desc: Advertising executive Carol Templeton is determined to outdo her competitor Jerry Webster at every opportunity. When an important campaign comes up, she tries to beat him to it and clinch the deal, whatever the price. Unfortunately for her, she fails to realise the whole thing is a set-up devised by her hated rival. Romantic comedy, starring Doris Day, Rock Hudson, Tony Randall, Edie Adams and Jack Oakie

programme.title: Pillow Talk (1959)
programme.desc: A man and woman who have never met squabble about each others use of a shared telephone line. One day their paths cross by chance and he is instantly smitten with her. Quickly realising who she really is, he poses as a perfect gentleman from the South to woo her. Romantic comedy, starring Rock Hudson, Doris Day, Tony Randall, Thelma Ritter, Nick Adams and Julia Meade

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team visit Hopetoun House not far from Edinburgh. Antiques experts James Lewis and Adam Partridge are on hand to value items, and Paul discovers the architectural history of the venue and why it is such a unique treasure in the Scottish countryside

programme.title: Live Irish League Football
programme.sub-title: Glentoran v Linfield - League Cup Final
programme.desc: Glentoran v Linfield (Kick-off 3.00pm). Stephen Watson presents coverage of the BetMcLean Cup Final from Clearer Twist National Stadium at Windsor Park. The Glens will hope to go one better than last year when they were beaten finalists in this competition, losing 1-0 to Cliftonville after extra time, but to do so theyll have to beat the team that has lifted this trophy more than any other, while The Blues will hope to add to their 12 previous titles, the most recent of which came two seasons ago. With commentary by Thomas Kane

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and tour guide Paul Gorton test Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Gladiator Apollo, on their knowledge of the UK, beginning with Derry/Londonderry

programme.title: The Chronicles of Armagh
programme.desc: Ranger Donald witnesses the start of the deer breeding season and Jane gathers the autumn harvest from the orchard. At the Crooked Lake, Rosie takes satisfaction from a 2.5km open water swim

programme.title: Six Nations Rugby Special
programme.desc: Ugo Monye is joined by Sam Warburton and John Barclay to present action from the final round of fixtures, featuring France v England at Stade de France, Wales v Italy at Principality Stadium and Ireland v Scotland at Aviva Stadium. France were looking to wrap-up another strong campaign in style, while Wales were bidding for a first home win in the competition since 2022, and Scotland were hoping to end an 11-match losing streak against the Irish

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Arran, Callum has to gather their flock from 4,000 acres of steep, rough hill terrain, while Zara recalls the devastating effects of his quadbike accident on that same ground. In Dornoch, Rob and Emma need the vets help when a ewe gets into trouble birthing triplets, while Ally and daughter Bethany travel by boat to gather sheep on an uninhabited island to bring them home to their croft on Lewis

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Abandoned by their dominant males, the Xudum pride comes under siege from more intruder lions, and the mums with young cubs must link up with the rest of the pride for protection. Leopard mum Lediba struggles after a lion steals her kill. To feed her cub, she is forced to make a dangerous move. Pobe is teaching cheetah cubs Kea and Bo to hunt, but when a hyena pack arrives, their survival instincts are pushed to the limit. With predators all around, every mother must fight harder than ever to keep her young alive

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: In the early hours a frantic teenage girl calls 999 to say that her friend has been attacked and stabbed in a car park. Police rush to the scene, where they find a 16-year-old with a life-threatening wound to his stomach. While his life hangs in the balance, West Midlands Polices homicide team get to work, with forensic investigators discovering two discarded weapons at the scene - a kitchen knife and a machete

programme.title: Cumasc: Seisiúin sa Black Gate
programme.sub-title: Niamh Regan and Caoimhín Ó Raghallaigh
programme.desc: A collaboration in which distinctive Galway singer-songwriter Niamh Regan creates new music with Dublin fiddler and composer Caoimhín Ó Raghallaigh. In Irish

programme.title: Cumasc: Seisiúin sa Black Gate
programme.desc: Galway multi-instrumentalist, composer and singer-songwriter Naomi Berrill collaborates with west Cork singer-songwriter Molly OMahony. In Irish

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Host Ed Gamble warms up the audience at Londons Hammersmith Apollo and then introduces routines by fellow stand-up comedians Tom Davis and Judi Love, who share their light-hearted views on modern life

programme.title: The Chronicles of Strangford
programme.desc: It is summer and Mark is camping out in the hope of seeing some early morning bird activity, while Katie is searching for barn owl chicks

programme.title: Zero (2022)
programme.desc: In a dystopian near future, the Gateway offers the only chance of survival for teenagers Zero and Serger and their younger sister Wake, who they are determined to save from the violence of the City. When Zeros life is threatened, she flees with Wake, while Serger is forced to chase them down. Faye Gilberts thriller, starring Lauren Grace

programme.title: Small Prophets
programme.desc: Strange things are happening in Marvin Gardens and they are starting to attract unwanted attention. Gordon threatens to fire Michael, while Brian has received a letter from an old friend, and a hole in the roof leads to a discovery. Kacey and Michael exhume the prophets from their pile of manure to see if they have reached the state of divination and the ability to predict the future. Comedy written and directed by Mackenzie Crook, starring Pearce Quigley and Lauren Patel

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, with Labours deputy leader Lucy Powell; the Conservative Shadow Business Minister Harriett Baldwin; the journalist Kay Burley; the Green Partys Sian Berry and the Times columnist Fraser Nelson facing questions from the audience in Wythenshawe

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from Detling antiques fair, in Kent. Catherine Southon helps mother-and-daughter duo Fiona and Millie on the red team while Jonathan Pratt guides married couple Carlie and Martin in blue as they search for bargains to sell at auction

programme.title: Dragons Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Steven Bartlett are joined by beauty mogul Jenna Meek as they grill the latest business hopefuls. Pitching to the Dragons this time - a founder from Northern Ireland presents her snack range designed to unleash the natural power of oats. A father-and-son duo unveil their lockdown-born DIY gadget. A software developer from London brings a real-world hide-and-seek adventure with a tech twist. And a physiologist from Lancashire hopes to turn heads with her protective, sun-shielding lip care range that doubles as everyday cosmetic skincare

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Tottenham Hotspur at Anfield, and Manchester United v Aston Villa at Old Trafford. Liverpool won 2-1 against a Spurs side that ended the game with nine men in the reverse fixture, but the Londoners will have been determined to avoid another defeat to ease their relegation concerns. Villa beat United by the same score when they last met, with Morgan Rogers scoring a goal in either half to inspire them to victory. Plus, Crystal Palace v Leeds United, and Nottingham Forest v Fulham

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: The Hairy Bakers Shorts
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers bake white rolls in a Doncaster market then mill their own flour at a windmill in Lincolnshire to make a classic brown loaf for a ploughmans lunch

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: James Crawford examines how aerial photography has revealed Scotlands hidden ancient past and uncovered secrets buried beneath the ground, including the discovery of hundreds of pizza ovens that once fed Romes mighty legions, and the outline of a lost ornamental garden

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the antiques team value finds in Selby, North Yorkshire, turning up Masonic porcelain, a picture embroidered from sock-wool in memory of the gallant Captain Oates and a personalised copy of Beatrix Potters book The Fairy Caravan

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Adam Partridge travel to Bangor, Gwynedd, where they value items including a collection of toys and a mirror, while Paul Martin learns about medieval musical instrument the crwth, which is experiencing a resurgence in popularity

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Steve Backshall, Steve Pemberton, Sam Quek and Rachel Riley face off, demonstrating the general knowledge and trivia skills that made them champions last time

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Host Vernon Kay and unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton test Susan Calman, Max Fosh and Noreen Khan on their knowledge of the UK, beginning with Dundee

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Investigations from BBC correspondents all over the world, revealing secrets, rooting out injustice and exposing crime, corruption and abuse

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotlands Lochs
programme.desc: Leaving Kinlochleven, Paul Murton discovers a forgotten First World War German prison camp, before coming across a colossal memorial in concrete to the last of the navvies. After failing miserably at clay pigeon shooting at Corrour Lodge, Paul makes a long hike down to Loch Laggan, where he is taken out to an island with a legendary feasting tradition

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie hosts the second semi-final, asking questions on the specialist subjects of Jeff Buckley, the managerial career of Rafael Benítez, Andy Warhol, and the classic Routemaster bus., before the contestants are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Amol Rajan asks the questions as two teams who lost their first match return to play a second quarter-final, with both needing to win this one to stay in the competition

programme.title: Inside the Rage Machine
programme.desc: The BBCs social media investigations correspondent Marianna Spring uncovers the inside story of how social media algorithms designed to connect people have been helping to tear them apart. Insider testimony and whistleblower documents expose a machine thriving on outrage and division as part of a business model, with radicalisation, real-world violence and fractured societies some of the consequences of a system built to shape how users think, feel and see the world

programme.title: Cumasc: Seisiúin sa Black Gate
programme.desc: Co Clare songwriter, vocalist and multi-instrumentalist Susan ONeill collaborates with Phil Christie, a Dublin composer, multi-instrumentalist, improviser and performer. In Irish

programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Small Prophets
programme.desc: The bottled prophets are looking unwell and Michael still hasnt asked his questions. Brian is causing havoc at the nursing home and warns Michael and Kacey that time is of the essence, but on their return, all hell has broken out in Marvin Gardens., with the cul-de-sac crammed full of vehicles, strangers and irate neighbours

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Ed Byrne, Jimmy Carr and Judi Love join regular panellist Alan Davies on the comedy quiz, with host Sandi Toksvig asking questions on the subject of vocal

programme.title: American Fiction (2024)
programme.desc: Frustrated novelist Monk is fed up with the establishment profiting from black entertainment that relies on tired and offensive tropes. To prove his point, he uses a pseudonym to write his own outlandish book, which propels him into the heart of hypocrisy and the madness he claims to disdain. Satire, starring Jeffrey Wright

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Vick Hope explore sugar beet harvesting in the east of England, 100 years after sugar was first grown in this country. In Norfolk, Vick meets farmers racing to lift the last of this years crop, while Charlotte visits one of the countrys biggest processing plants. Plus, Tom Heap investigates whats really in the sewage sludge spread as fertiliser

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and her team of experts are back to help more families declutter their homes. They begin with the Kings in Fife - retired architectural technician Gerry, care worker Trish and two of their three grown-up children. Their house contains 30 years of memories, knick-knacks and general clutter - not to mention countless unfinished DIY projects. During the process, the team learn Gerry has recently been diagnosed with early onset dementia, so it is important they hold on to items that will continue to spark memories for him while losing any clutter so it is easy for him to move around

programme.title: UFOs: The White House Files

programme.title: Alien Disclosure Files
programme.desc: Several US presidents throughout history have described encounters with Unidentified Aerial Phenomenas. Is there already an agreement in place between world leaders and intelligent alien life? And if so, who is keeping that information from the public and why?

programme.title: Mothering Sunday (2021)
programme.desc: A maid living in post-First World War England secretly plans to meet with the man she loves while her employers are away, seizing a last chance to be together before he leaves to marry another woman. Romantic drama, starring Odessa Young, Josh OConnor, Colin Firth and Olivia Colman

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Theres fireworks ahead as some of the cuffed pairs grapple with being chained to their total opposite. Horse trainer and countryside campaigner Claire, whos 48, is a fierce advocate of hard work. Her handcuffed partner, 29-year-old city-loving model and content creator Bambi loves the easy life. Can Bambi keep up with Claires exhausting work schedule? In London, Green Party councillor Frank, whos 27, and housing lawyer Lin, whos 38, seem to be getting along famously. But Franks in for a shock that leaves him speechless

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Some of the pairs have had enough and called it quits. But not 70-year-old Yorkshireman Bob and 38-year-old London youth worker Chris. However, when the latter learns about a shocking incident from his partners past, he wonder whether he can continue. Meanwhile, eco-warrior and vegan campaigner Morag is shocked when the woman she is chained to, petrol-head and beauty salon owner Angie, reveals her views on climate change. Hosted by Jonathan Ross

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Single mother Jo Beck wants to move her family to Derby, despite her children being happily settled in Nottingham. Phil Spencer helps her decide if relocating is the right choice and searches for a suitable home. Also in Derby, Kirstie Allsopp offers advice to outdoor sports enthusiasts Liz and John Fairfield, who are looking for a property with a large garden, storage space for their sporting equipment and no noise from nearby roads

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes a union representative at the power plant and immediately uses his new-found authority to save the companys free dental plan and avoid having to pay for Lisas new braces. Featuring the voice of Dr Joyce Brothers

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: So Its Come to This: A Simpsons Clip Show
programme.desc: Barts latest prank wrecks the house and lands Homer in hospital, where he and the family reminisce about all the other injuries and mishaps he has suffered in his eventful and disaster-filled life

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Part one of two. Carrie discovers she is expecting a baby. Deacon and Kelly make a move to reconcile, leaving Spence out on a limb, and Arthur asks Doug to build him a bathroom in the basement. Comedy, starring Kevin James and Leah Remini

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Part two of two. Doug takes on a second job as a chauffeur to supplement his income, pressured by the expense of the new baby and bathroom. Meanwhile, Deacon and Kelly are reconciled with each other - leaving Spence all alone. Comedy, starring Kevin James

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge puts a screen-time limit on the rest of the family, but soon realises that she might be the one who is addicted

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart starts to bond with Ned, prompting a jealous Homer to decide to start mentoring Nelson in retaliation

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and Billy Monger present coverage of the ninth and final day of the Games from Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. The first run of the mens vision impaired alpine skiing slalom final takes place at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, where ParalympicsGB stars Neil Simpson, Sam Cozens and Fred Warburton are expected to be in action. Attention will then turn to the Tesero Cross-Country Skiing Stadium where a number of para cross-country skiing finals take place, including the mens 20km interval start sitting event, where Scott Meenagh is likely to bring his career to an end. Meenagh was a Scotland U18 international rugby player before losing his legs after stepping on an IED in Afghanistan at the age of 21, and went on to become the first Briton to win a medal in para biathlon, claiming silver in the 12.5km individual event at the 2023 World Para Nordic Skiing Championships. With reports from Alice Tai and Dame Sarah Storey

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Concluding coverage of the ninth and final day of the Games from Milan and Cortina dAmpezzo, Italy. Medals will be decided in the second run of the mens vision impaired alpine skiing slalom final at Tofane Alpine Skiing Centre, with British stars Neil Simpson, Sam Cozens, and Fred Warburton all hoping for a place on the podium. Elsewhere, the para ice hockey competition is brought to a conclusion with the bronze- and gold-medal matches at Milano Santagiulia Ice Hockey Arena, the latter of which is the final action of the Games. With reports from Alice Tai and Dame Sarah Storey

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.desc: Lee McKenzie is joined by David Coulthard, Damon Hill, and Ariana Bravo to present action from the second round of the season at Shanghai International Circuit, where McLaren drivers Oscar Piastri and Lando Norris finished first and second last season. While the race itself proved to be relatively uneventful, the aftermath was anything but, with Charles Leclerc of Ferrari and Pierre Gasly of Alpine being disqualified due to their cars being underweight, Leclercs teammate Lewis Hamilton also being disqualified for excessive skid wear, and In the days that followed, Red Bull announcing that Liam Lawson would undergo a driver swap with Racing Bulls Yuki Tsunoda

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first destination of the week is the Horseshoe Hotel in Egton Bridge, North Yorkshire, where a fancy machine causes confusion but breakfast and eco toiletries prove a big hit

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB battle continues in Market Bosworth at the Dixie Arms, run by Alex, whose blingy beds impress two of the guests. However, not all of the facilities are quite as successful

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Bradburys of Blackpool, where former flight attendant Matt is hoping to change his guests perceptions of the seaside town

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to Little Oakhurst, in Stonyhurst near Clitheroe, run by husband-and-wife team Andy and Joanne. The guests are immediately captivated by the stunning location, with views over the Lancashire countryside, though the optional and additional breakfast brings some mixed reviews. At feedback time, it becomes clear that glamping isnt for everyone

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment Day sees Craig and Greta keen to get to the bottom of facilities faults, before Alex points the finger at them about some unsavoury feedback scores. Matt wants to question Alexs cleanliness comments, and Joanne and Andy have some beef over their breakfast scores

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: In Dartmouth, Nigel Cooper sails his steamboat Halcyon along the River Dart for the clubs annual competition. His boat has won twice in the past - can he make it a hat-trick? In Cornwall, farrier and horseman David Jones is hoping his age-old skills will be passed down the line to daughter Myrtle, as they head out to tackle a rampant bracken problem in an old woodland. And in Cornwalls Trebah Gardens, head gardener Darren Dickey embarks on a new project. To celebrate 30 years of the garden becoming a public trust, hes creating a new Victorian-style stumpery with an array of ferns to delight visitors

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan, Billy Monger, Ed Jackson, Sarah Storey, Sean Rose and Alice Tai present live coverage of the event from Cortina dAmpezzo, as the Paralympic flame is extinguished, signaling the end of the 14th Winter Games. Athletes, artists and spectators from all over the world gather in the Cortina Olympic Curling Stadium to enjoy a spectacular final show. The presenting team look back at all the standout stories and key moments from nine days of elite competition spanning six sports

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on The Capture, Married at First Sight Australia, Louis Theroux: Inside the Manosphere, The Floor, Will and Ralf Should Know Better, Britains Best Mattress: How to Sleep Better and This Morning (AI boyfriend)

programme.title: The Son (2022)
programme.desc: New York lawyer Peter Miller receives a panicked visit from his ex-wife Kate. Their 17-year-old son Nicholas has been playing truant from school for a month. Peter allows Nicholas to move in with him but the teenager continues to suffer, unable to articulate the darkness that consumes him. Drama, starring Hugh Jackman, Vanessa Kirby and Anthony Hopkins

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Los Angeles to help La Frite, an outdated French restaurant that is failing to make any profits. His first step is to try to reconcile the feuding family members who run the business, and so devises a plan to bring them together by using secret filming

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa tries to persuade her family not to take part in the towns annual snake-clubbing ritual, but her words fall on deaf ears as Homer anticipates the event with glee. With the guest voice of Barry White

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Long-standing TV personality Krusty the Clown reaches the end of the road, his days of entertaining kids quashed by an alternative show - prompting his famous friends to come to the rescue. Animation, with the voices of Elizabeth Taylor, Hugh Hefner, Bette Midler and the Red Hot Chili Peppers

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Cambridge, the first hosts are 23-year-old stable owner Alice and her feisty boyfriend Ollie, who reveals that he helped to hunt and butcher the deer theyre serving for their main course. Travel specialists Gareth and Hihine try to throw a more civilised evening, based around a generations-old family Asian menu. On the last night, John and Katy aim to spread the love among the group by serving up the same menu that they had for their wedding six weeks earlier, while John wears his wedding suit and Katy wears half her wedding dress

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Keith and Marj focus on the last thing they need to do to finally call Capel Salem home: bringing the bedroom inside the building! Marj gilds a vintage French mirror, while Keith takes on storage solutions and they both reflect on their journey so far by visiting a Welsh love spoon workshop

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer assist two couples in their search for homes in Hertfordshire. Newly engaged accountants Liz and Neil are hoping to raise a family near the town of Tring, while in St Albans, married couple David and Claire want to find a place where they can finally settle down, after moving house five times in five years

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Colin Jackson joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: Do Not Forsake Me, O My Postman
programme.desc: An old flame of Cliffs makes a dramatic return to the bar, claiming to be pregnant with his child, and Rebecca recruits a jingle writer to improve business. Featuring an appearance by John Mahoney, later one of the stars of spin-off Frasier

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part one of two. Frasier is devastated to learn Lilith has betrayed him with a colleague, while Sam hires a bouncer. Bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: The Girl in the Plastic Bubble
programme.desc: Lilith tells Frasier she plans to spend a year in a biosphere with her lover - leaving the traumatised psychiatrist teetering precariously on a window ledge. Bar-room comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Bebe Neuwirth

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: As a challenge, Ray writes a moving eulogy for Frank - despite the obvious fact he is still alive. Ray Romano and Peter Boyle star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray thinks something is wrong and confides in Debra when he has a miserable night with his friends, but an invitation to join Frank for a steam bath restores his spirits. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Peter Boyle

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra give Robert $1,000 to help him out of dire financial straits - but he takes advantage of their generosity by jetting off for a holiday in Las Vegas

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Samantha ends her relationship with Frasier on their return from a seemingly perfect weekend away, leaving him desperate to find out where he went wrong. However, Niles helps him realise that he only liked his new woman for her famous friends. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A story about the holiday season is told from the perspectives of Daphne, Niles, Martin and Roz as the Crane family prepares for Christmas. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, John Mahoney and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: Where Every Bloke Knows Your Name
programme.desc: A British theme pub becomes Frasiers favourite new haunt - much to the annoyance of Daphne, who is already a regular and dislikes having to share the place with her boss. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink has to choose whether to return to his heartless former agent Bebe, or stick with his current representative, well-meaning but ineffectual Ben. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to help the owners of Iberville Cuisine and Oyster Bar, in the heart of the French Quarter in New Orleans. They want to be reinvigorated before Super Bowl LIX kicks off in the citys Caesars Superdome - and the tourist trade that comes with hosting the biggest sporting event in America

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Midday news summary

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to Tiverton in the West Country to meet petrolhead Roger. Henry finds part of a tornado jet that he wants to turn into a table, while Simon finds a pair of exhaust pipes, which he thinks could become a trendy pair of lamps

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: Documentary following the Countryfile presenter as he and his family come to the rescue his parents farm in the Durham hills after his mother suffers an accident

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Nicki Chapman in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps Mandy and Mick, acouple who dream of owning a holiday home on the sunny southern Costa Blanca, that they can move to permanently once they are both retired

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Renovation rookies Angie and Ash dream big in the Italian Alps. In France, neighbours Aggi and Camille join forces to turn around a derelict restaurants fortunes. Sparks fly in Portugal, as Anglo-American couple Harriet and Mondo fight to boost business at their holiday cottages

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first venue of the week is Hotel Penzance in Cornwall, where owners Louise and Daga hope to wow their guests with breathtaking views over St Michaels Mount. An afternoon on the water brings with it revelations that make waves, and comments about TV troubles get a bad reception

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Teaching assistant Michelle hosts the first party from Bedfordshire and Cambridgeshire, hoping to share her Portuguese and American heritage as well as dishes from Italy

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: What Happened Next?
programme.desc: The presenters catch up with house-hunters who bought their perfect place abroad. Jean Johansson revisits Lisa and Rob in southwest Frances Charente region, while Jasmine Harman heads to the Andalucian countryside of Velez-Málaga to see how Alison and her daughter Paula are getting on

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Political Slot
programme.desc: Danny Kruger, MP for East Wiltshire, sets out Reform UKs plans to provide more support for Britains veterans

programme.title: Batch from Scratch: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Suzanne Mulholland and Joe Swash help a single mother in Aberdeen, who is struggling to get her kids into healthy eating habits, regularly prepares multiple meals, and often doesnt have time to sit down at the table to eat herself. Suzanne and Joe create a plan to cut costs, reduce sugar, simplify dinnertimes and get everyone involved, kids included

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: With the final getting ever closer, the handcuffed couples face a new challenge: their first holiday away together. Jonathan Ross sends them across the UK on iconic British breaks, from beautiful canal trips to camping in the wild countryside, and enjoying all the fun of the fair at the great British seaside. On neutral territory, and without friends and family around, how do they cope with just their cuff-partner for company?

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Sisters Caitlin and Lucy have never been in a romantic relationship with a guy. They decide to visit the restaurant to each take a dive into dating. Naked cleaner Elleigha is confident in everything - except romance. Shes matched with head chef Louis, a man searching for a wild connection with someone with strong opinions. Aspiring erotic-fiction-writer Immogen also attends, finally ready to live out her passions in real life

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Sarah from Plymouth becomes the first returning picker having previously been on the show as David. Now proudly pansexual and living as a woman, Sarah cant wait to road test her new body and hopes her first date as a woman will be everything she has dreamed of. Twenty-year-old plumber Jamie from Swansea is fed up with being hit on by OAPs at work, he comes into the studio looking for love

programme.title: The Eclipse
programme.desc: Mystery drama set in the Aubrac region of France, focusing on events taking place in the picturesque village of Bozouls. The community is buzzing with activity. Manue and Johanna, both gendarmes, get together for the big party to celebrate the eclipse. However tragedy strikes when Nour, the daughter of new rural settlers, disappears after a terrible accident. Manue and Johanna investigate the disappearance, which involves their children. With Anne Charrier, Claire Keim and Hubert Delattre. In French

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.sub-title: Lido, Anna Vincenzos and Le Bistro
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three of the restaurants he tried to save, returning to Lido, Anna Vincenzos and Le Bistro to find out whether his advice has been put to good use

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: Brenda Blethyn and Jessica Reynolds star in this drama based on the bestseller by Barbara Taylor Bradford, charting a womans rise from maid to businesswoman. In early 1900s Yorkshire, ambitious maid Emma Harte dreams of escaping Fairley Hall for a better life, but is caught up in a forbidden romance with Edwin Fairley, son of the master of the house. In 1970s New York, the worlds richest woman is warned that a plot is brewing to take away the empire she has worked so hard to build. With Ewan Horrocks, Will Mellor, Leanne Best and Lydia Leonard
